items.headline: US Futures Mixed Amid Geopolitical Tensions and Inflation Watch
items.summary: US equity futures are signaling a mixed to lower open on Thursday, May 28, as investors weigh ongoing geopolitical tensions between the US and Iran against a backdrop of key inflation data releases. This follows a strong Wednesday session where all three major US indexes closed at record highs. Market sentiment remains cautious, influenced by rising oil prices due to the Strait of Hormuz closure and the Federal Reserve's commitment to taming persistent inflation. However, robust corporate earnings, particularly from the technology and artificial intelligence sectors, continue to provide underlying support to the market.

items.headline: S&P 500
items.statContext: Edged up 0.02% to another record close.. Futures down 0.1% on Thursday.

items.headline: NASDAQ Composite
items.statContext: Added 0.07% to a record close.. Futures down 0.3% on Thursday.

items.headline: Dow Jones Industrial Average
items.statContext: Rose 182.60 points (+0.36%) to a fresh intraday and closing high.. Futures down 0.1% on Thursday.

items.headline: Snowflake Surges on AWS Spending & Earnings Beat
items.summary: Snowflake surged in extended trading on Wednesday after announcing plans to spend US$6 billion on Amazon Web Services over five years, coupled with quarterly earnings and revenue that exceeded expectations.

items.headline: Micron Soars on Strong Revenue Growth
items.summary: Micron Technology ($MU) shares surged by 5% today after the company reported a remarkable 20% increase in revenue year-over-year, driven by heightened demand for memory chips essential for various digital applications.

items.headline: AppLovin Leads Gainers
items.summary: AppLovin ($APP) showed strong performance among US markets today, leading gainers.

items.headline: Sidus Space Drops on Direct Offering
items.summary: Sidus Space ($SIDU) shares fell significantly in early premarket trade after the company announced a $100 million registered direct offering.

items.headline: Verra Mobility Sees Sharp Decline
items.summary: Verra Mobility Corporation ($VRRM) experienced a significant decline, losing over 70% of its value (data updated May 27, 2026).

items.headline: Zscaler Continues Slide
items.summary: Cloud security firm Zscaler ($ZS) continued its decline, with a notable drop (data updated May 27, 2026).

items.headline: Technology and AI Drive Market Gains, Utilities Lag
items.summary: Technology and artificial intelligence-linked sectors are continuing to show strong momentum, driving overall market gains, bolstered by robust earnings from semiconductor giants. Conversely, the utilities sector has been identified as a significant laggard for May, potentially due to increasing concerns about the impact of AI data centers on power grids and water supplies. The energy sector, while a strong performer year-to-date, has seen its momentum stall.

items.headline: Key Economic Data Releases: PCE, GDP, and More
items.summary: Today, May 28, 2026, marks a busy day for economic data releases, including the Personal Consumption Expenditures (PCE) price index – a crucial inflation indicator – and the second estimate for Q1 GDP. Additionally, personal income, personal spending, initial jobless claims, durable goods orders, and new home sales are all scheduled for release.

items.headline: New Fed Chair Kevin Warsh Confronts Inflationary Challenges
items.summary: Kevin Warsh, sworn in as the new Federal Reserve Chair on May 22, faces an immediate challenge of combating an 'inflationary mess' attributed to ongoing price shocks, including new tariffs and the US-Iran conflict. His tenure is expected to focus heavily on taming inflation. Minneapolis Fed President Neel Kashkari also reiterated the Fed's commitment to inflation control today.

items.headline: Major Companies Report Earnings: Dell, Costco, Hormel
items.summary: Several major companies are reporting earnings today. Dell Technologies Inc. ($DELL) and Costco Wholesale Corporation ($COST) are scheduled to release their Q3 FY22 results after market close. Hormel Foods Corporation ($HRL) reported strong Q2 fiscal 2026 results, with net sales up 3% organically and diluted EPS of $0.29. Dollar Tree, Inc. ($DLTR) also reported Q1 results and provided Q2 EPS guidance. Overall, Q1 earnings season is concluding with 84% of S&P 500 companies beating estimates.
